snakeATAC.utility 1.0.0

  • Articles
    • *snakeATAC.utility* overview

subset_BINDetect

Source: R/subset.BINDetect.R
subset.BINDetect.Rd

Subset TOBIAS BINDetect results to regions overlapping a BED file. Re-summarises a single BINDetect condition-comparison, restricting the analysis to the transcription-factor binding sites (TFBS) that overlap a user-supplied set of regions. It reads the per-TF <TF>_overview.txt files produced by TOBIAS BINDetect, keeps the sites overlapping bed, and recomputes per-TF summary statistics (mean/median footprint scores, bound counts, and a differential-binding effect) together with a significance test against a background distribution of per-site log2 fold changes.

# S3 method for class 'BINDetect'
subset(
  bindetect_dir,
  bed,
  condition1 = NULL,
  condition2 = NULL,
  bound_in_any = FALSE,
  background = c("all", "outside"),
  summary_stat = c("mean", "median"),
  min_sites = 10L,
  top_quantile = 0.05,
  padjust_method = "BH"
)

Arguments

bindetect_dir

Path to the BINDetect output directory for one comparison, i.e. the folder that contains bindetect_results.txt and one sub-directory per TF (e.g. 05b_Differential_TF_binding_TOBIAS/C_BINDetect_merged_BAMs/groupA.vs.groupB).

bed

Path to a BED file (or a data.frame/GRanges) with the regions of interest to which the analysis should be restricted.

condition1, condition2

Character scalars with the two condition names, exactly as passed to --cond_names (used to locate the <cond>_score, <cond>_bound and <cond1>_<cond2>_log2fc columns). If NULL (default) they are inferred from the header of bindetect_results.txt.

bound_in_any

Logical. If TRUE, keep only sites predicted bound in at least one condition before summarising. Note this biases the log2fc distribution (it removes unbound-unbound sites, which sit near zero) and therefore your top-quantile selection; exposed as an option rather than hard-coded. Default FALSE.

background

Which sites form the null for the significance test: "all" (default) uses the pooled per-site log2fc of every TF genome-wide (mirrors the "true background = full peak set" logic); "outside" uses, per TF, that TF's sites not overlapping bed (answers the different question "is this TF's differential binding specific to my regions?").

summary_stat

Central tendency for the per-TF effect, one of "mean" or "median" of the subset per-site log2fc. Default "mean".

min_sites

Minimum number of subset sites required to test a TF; TFs below this get NA p-values. Default 10.

top_quantile

Tail fraction used to flag "top" TFs, mirroring the BINDetect volcano (effect in the lower/upper top_quantile tails and/or -log10(padj) above the upper tail). Default 0.05.

padjust_method

Passed to p.adjust. Default "BH".

Value

A data.frame, one row per TF, with columns: TF, n_sites_total, n_sites_subset, <cond>_mean_score, <cond>_bound, subset_change (the chosen central tendency of the subset log2fc; negative = higher in condition2), pvalue, padj and top (logical). Sorted by padj then abs(subset_change).

Important statistical note

In TOBIAS BINDetect, the *_change and *_pvalue columns of bindetect_results.txt are not self-contained per-TF quantities: the between-condition score normalisation, the bound/unbound threshold and the null model for the p-values are all estimated from the whole peak set. Restricting to a sub-region and rebuilding the background from that subset therefore skews the distribution (see loosolab/TOBIAS issue #48). This function instead keeps the per-site log2 fold changes exactly as BINDetect computed them (they are already normalised between conditions) and, by default, tests each TF's subset against a genome-wide background (background = "all"). If your regions are large (>500) and you want a properly re-centred volcano, prefer re-running BINDetect with your BED as --peaks (see rerun_bindetect_on_bed).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
res = subset_bindetect_by_bed(
  bindetect_dir = "05b_Differential_TF_binding_TOBIAS/C_BINDetect_merged_BAMs/groupA.vs.groupB",
  bed = "my_regions.bed",
  condition1 = "groupA",
  condition2 = "groupB")
head(res[res$top, ])
} # }
